# Slice 010 contract evidence manifest (T018; regenerated by T042, attempt 3)

**Recorded on**: 2026-07-18, directly in the `v2-contract-owner` worktree by
cc-session-1 (not via a bound `run speckit` invocation — see
`evidence/v2/contract/checklist-adjudication.md`'s attempt-3 adjudication
header for why).

**Attempt rework disposition (CHK089, generalized by T035's CHK107 fix)**:
this manifest and all three aggregate JSONL files are current-attempt
records, regenerated in place after the R4 rework changed every schema and
every corpus family; the attempt-2 versions remain recoverable from git
history at the rejected candidate commit
`5383e9f3a5e9c20c08ab54395f4ff370128f03de`. Unlike the attempt-2 rework,
`attention-request.jsonl` is **not** unchanged this attempt — R4 re-enters
every corpus family (FR-014 selected-design fidelity), so all three files
are re-recorded as current-attempt records.

**What changed (R4, attempt 3)**: reading the pinned selected design at
`c834e8c` directly (`projects/shared/nunchi/technical-design.md` in the
`aleph-vault` repository) rather than relying on the spec's own FR-014
summary surfaced that the attempt-2 schemas' envelope itself diverged from
the design — a defect neither the attempt-1 nor attempt-2 integrator review
had caught. All five schemas were reworked: the request drops the invented
`interface`/`version` tags for the design's own `schema_version: 2`; `actors`
becomes a map keyed by actor ID (not an array); `room` gains
`platform`/`id`/`continuity_scope_id`/`name`/`kind`; events become a typed
`message`/`reaction`/`membership` union keyed by `type`/`id`; `coverage`
gains `has_more_before`/`has_more_after`/`has_gaps`/`truncated_by`/
`has_restart_gap`/optional `event_visibility`; the request MAY carry the
full `continuation` capability (the design's own example embeds it — FR-004
was corrected to move the classifier-facing redaction to runtime, not the
wire schema); the decision drops `interface`/`version`, renames
`routing`→`routing_audit` and `classifier_audit`→`classifier` (reshaped to
`name`/`provider?`/`model?`), `legacy_confidence`→`legacy_verdict_confidences`,
and `advice`→`attention_advice` (an array of `{note, evidence_event_ids}`,
not a single object); the wake packet materializes self/room/actors/events/
trigger/coverage/continuation directly with a separate `attention` object
(`source`/`advice?`/`evidence_event_ids?`), dropping the wrapped
`observation` reference and the separate `budgets` field; the continuation
interface drops `interface`/`version`/`kind` and the inline `binding` object
in favor of the design's bare `ContextFetch`/`ContextPage` fields
(`handle_id`, `direction`, `anchor_event_id`, `cursor`, `max_events`,
`max_bytes` / `room_id`, `continuity_scope_id`, `actors`, `events`,
`coverage`, `next_cursor`); the receipt's stage bodies were reworked to the
selected telemetry (observation: `schema_version`/`trigger_event_id`/
`continuity_scope_id`/`event_count`/`byte_count`/`coverage`/
`included_event_ids`; attention: `classifier_disposition`/
`effective_disposition`/`classifier`/`evidence_event_ids`/`routing_audit`,
or `error: {code, detail}`, or the flattened bypass
`classifier_not_invoked`/`cause`/`policy_provenance`; participant-host:
`wake_source`/`packet_event_count`/`packet_byte_count`/`delivered_event_ids`/
`expansion_calls`/`invoked`/`outcome` with outcome now `sent`/`silent`/
`unknown`, not `contributed`/`silence`). The stdlib runtime adapter, all
four test files, and all three corpora were reworked to match. The removed
fetch-time "changed binding" check (`context-continuation`'s `ContextFetch`
carries no inline binding fields per the design) reduces `downstream`'s
`binding-expiry` invalid count from 4 to 3 (CHK107 disposition: an
intentional, primary-source-driven reduction, not a silent shrink — the
removed check tested a field that no longer exists in the selected shape).

Each aggregate JSONL evidence file holds two records per corpus case — one
per validator — and every record carries the five mandatory fields
(`scene_id`, `case_id`, `validator`, `expected`, `observed`). A record ID
below is the case's `case_id`; the two records for a case share it and are
distinguished by the `validator` field.

## Commands and results

The exact offline dual-validator command (the sole complete run):

```sh
uv run --offline --with 'jsonschema==4.26.0' python -m unittest discover -s tests/v2/contract -p 'test_*.py'
```

Result (2026-07-18, post-rework tree): `Ran 164 tests ... OK`, 0 skipped —
every oracle-side check ran; only the explicit per-class oracle skips
applied inside the corpus runner (counted below).

The boundary-only SC-006 verification, run with no flags:

```sh
python3 scripts/check_governance.py
```

Result (2026-07-18): `governance boundary: OK (SpecKit 0.12.11)` — zero
product schemas, tests, fixtures, evaluation assets, evidence, or product
documentation under the SpecKit directories.

The full offline repository baseline (`python3 -m unittest`, full suite,
2026-07-18, post-rework tree): **1222 tests, OK, 11 skipped** — the 8
pre-existing V1 skips plus the 3 counted `baseline-oracle-absence` skips
(one per corpus suite). The test-count delta from the attempt-2 baseline
(1225 → 1222) is exactly three deliberate consolidations of red cases whose
tested field no longer exists under the selected design (attempt-2's
`attestation` field on `self`, and the fetch-request `binding` object) —
not a coverage loss; each removed case's scenario is superseded by the
corrected shape. Per CHK080 this recorded run is the one whose green result
the exact **candidate commit** must reproduce; the identical green
obligation for the exact **handoff packet commit** is owed as a rerun at
the handoff gate.

Evidence generation and shape verification:

```sh
uv run --offline --with 'jsonschema==4.26.0' python -m tests.v2.contract.schema_helpers --write-evidence
uv run --offline --with 'jsonschema==4.26.0' python -m tests.v2.contract.schema_helpers --verify-evidence
```

Result (2026-07-18): attention-request 80 records, attention-decision 130
records, downstream 134 records; 0 mismatched; all records carry the five
mandatory fields.

## Observed per-class partition counts (cases; each case = 2 records)

| Partition class | attention-request | attention-decision | downstream | Total |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `schema-expressible` | 34 | 63 | 50 | 147 |
| `id-uniqueness` | 2 | 0 | 2 | 4 |
| `timestamp-order` | 2 | 0 | 0 | 2 |
| `advice-citation` | 0 | 2 | 0 | 2 |
| `trigger-membership` | 2 | 0 | 0 | 2 |
| `binding-expiry` | 0 | 0 | 4 | 4 |
| `receipt-sequence` | 0 | 0 | 11 | 11 |
| **Total cases** | **40** | **65** | **67** | **172** |

These observed counts match each corpus's authoritative
`expected-counts.json` exactly (asserted loudly on every load). The
`schema-expressible` figures above include the FR-014 authority-conformance
class (14 cases total: 4 request, 4 decision, 6 downstream — enumerated
below), which is a named manifest-counted subset of `schema-expressible`,
never a fourth oracle-treatment class (CHK099/CHK115).

## Both skip regimes (kept separately named and counted)

| Regime | When | Count |
|---|---|---|
| `oracle-class-skip` | Under the pinned command: the oracle skips the two behavioral classes by explicit class | 15 cases (4 `binding-expiry` + 11 `receipt-sequence`, all in the downstream corpus; 15 oracle-side records observe `oracle-class-skip`) |
| `baseline-oracle-absence` | Under `python3 -m unittest` (no pinned oracle): every oracle-side check for the five oracle-visible classes is skipped with an explicit count | 157 oracle-side checks (40 attention-request + 65 attention-decision + 52 downstream), surfaced as 3 counted unittest skips |

## FR-014 authority-conformance class (CHK099/CHK110/CHK121; 14 cases)

Every case below is drawn verbatim or field-complete from the selected
design at `c834e8c` (`authority_source_commit: c834e8c` on each record
below), is schema-expressible (never a new partition class — CHK099), and
is recorded with its decisive red run against the attempt-2 packet commit
`5383e9f3a5e9c20c08ab54395f4ff370128f03de` (`red_run_failing_count` below,
CHK101/CHK119) alongside its green result at this commit (0 errors, per the
`write-evidence` run above).

| Case ID | Corpus | What it proves | `red_run_failing_count` |
|---|---|---|---|
| REQ-AUTH-001 | attention-request | the design's example attention request validates verbatim | 26 |
| REQ-AUTH-002 | attention-request | the typed reaction event union (add/remove operation) | 40 |
| REQ-AUTH-003 | attention-request | the typed membership event union (literal scope, subject/causal actor) | 36 |
| REQ-AUTH-004 | attention-request | the complete coverage field inventory incl. optional per-event-type visibility | 31 |
| DEC-AUTH-001 | attention-decision | a valid WAKE records routing-audit margin status + full classifier audit | 1 |
| DEC-AUTH-002 | attention-decision | a valid WAKE without a legacy verdict confidence vector, margin retired | 1 |
| DEC-AUTH-003 | attention-decision | the error branch's request ID is optional on a pre-validation error | 1 |
| DEC-AUTH-004 | attention-decision | the error branch's request ID/classifier audit on a post-validation error | 1 |
| DWN-AUTH-001 | downstream (context-continuation) | the directional anchored fetch (`around` + `anchor_event_id`) | 1 |
| DWN-AUTH-002 | downstream (context-continuation) | the identity-bearing page (room/continuity-scope identity, direction, anchor) | 1 |
| DWN-AUTH-003 | downstream (participant-wake) | the materialized wake packet, not a wrapped classifier projection | 6 |
| DWN-AUTH-004 | downstream (attention-receipt) | the observation-stage telemetry incl. `event_visibility` | 4 |
| DWN-AUTH-005 | downstream (attention-receipt) | the attention-stage telemetry (classifier identity, evidence, transition-valve) | 3 |
| DWN-AUTH-006 | downstream (attention-receipt) | the participant-host telemetry incl. `expansion_calls` | 4 |

`red_run_failing_count` values were measured directly: the attempt-2 schemas
were checked out at `5383e9f3a5e9c20c08ab54395f4ff370128f03de` into an
isolated scratch directory and each authority document was run through a
Draft 2020-12 validator built from those exact schema files. Decision and
continuation counts are `1` because `jsonschema`'s `oneOf`/`allOf` union
failure reports as a single top-level error with nested context (not
flattened); this is the oracle's genuine error-reporting behavior for a
tagged union, not an undercount.

DEC-S09-122 (twenty-two consecutive IDs, all present); every other row
enumerates its record IDs exhaustively. All 172 case IDs above (158 named
plus the abbreviated S09 run) are exactly the cases present in the three
corpora; no evidence record is outside this manifest.
